title: Analitiq canonical types
description:
Analitiq's profile of the Apache Arrow logical type system. Canonical types are strings that identify an Arrow logical type in the form used by `type_map.canonical` entries on connector definitions and `arrow_type` fields throughout the schema contracts.

Ownership: the ACCEPTED SET below is generated from the engine-published Arrow type grammar manifest (`https://schemas.analitiq.ai/arrow-type-grammar/latest.json`, pinned at v1.0.0) — the set of families the platform executes end-to-end. A family appears here only after the engine executes it; the contract adopts it by consuming the new manifest version, never by hand-editing this document.

Standard (one screen):

Base form: PascalCase Arrow type name from `arrow/format/Schema.fbs`.

Two parameter shapes:
  - Bare name, no params — scalar types and the authored-shape JSON container markers: `Utf8`, `Int64`, `Boolean`, `Date32`, `Binary`, `Object`, `List`, `Json`.
  - Parens `( )` for value params — parameterized scalars (units, precision/scale, byte widths): `Decimal128(38, 9)`, `Timestamp(MICROSECOND, UTC)`, `FixedSizeBinary(16)`.

Nested data is declared with the authored-shape markers only: `Object` / `List` carry their inner shape in sibling `properties` / `items` keys of the OWNING document (endpoint column, stream field spec), and `Json` is opaque. There are no fully-typed nested type strings in this vocabulary.

Unit values inside parens are the literal Flatbuffers enum identifiers — uppercase:
  - `TimeUnit`: `SECOND`, `MILLISECOND`, `MICROSECOND`, `NANOSECOND`.

Timezone (Timestamp only): optional second arg. `null`, IANA name (including `Etc/GMT±N`), or fixed `±HH:MM` offset.

Full canonical examples:
  Utf8
  Int64
  Boolean
  Date32
  Decimal128(38, 9)
  Decimal256(76, 0)
  Timestamp(MICROSECOND)
  Timestamp(MICROSECOND, UTC)
  Timestamp(MILLISECOND, +05:30)
  Time32(SECOND)
  Time64(NANOSECOND)
  Duration(MICROSECOND)
  FixedSizeBinary(16)
  Object
  List
  Json

Why uppercase unit names instead of PyArrow shorthand (`us`/`ms`/`ns`): the uppercase identifiers are the only spelling that's actually in the Arrow specification (the Flatbuffers `TimeUnit` enum). PyArrow's shortcodes are a Python-library convenience and read ambiguously in spec text (`us` parses as 'United States' to a casual reader or LLM). The engine tolerates the short forms on input; this authoring vocabulary does not. The translation to PyArrow constructor args is the runtime's job, not the spec's.

Why parens: parens hold values (integers, enums). The vocabulary has no type-parameter (angle-bracket) forms — nested shapes belong to the owning document's sibling keys, not to the type string.

Reference: https://arrow.apache.org/docs/format/Columnar.html#logical-types.

Scope note: `Decimal128/256` scale <= precision is a cross-parameter bound regex cannot express; the validator API and the contract models enforce it. Sibling-key rules for `Object`/`List`/`Json` are likewise the owning document's contract, not this string vocabulary's.

Version note: Arrow view types (`Utf8View`, `BinaryView`, `ListView`, `LargeListView`), the typed nested families (`List<T>`, `LargeList<T>`, `FixedSizeList<T>[n]`, `Struct<...>`, `Map<K, V>`), unions, encodings (`Dictionary`, `RunEndEncoded`), and `Interval` are not part of this canonical vocabulary: the platform does not execute them end-to-end. They return, if ever, by shipping in the engine first and re-consuming the grammar manifest (issue #81's re-add policy) — never by editing this document.
$comment: GENERATED by scripts/render_schemas.py from the vendored engine grammar manifest (analitiq.contracts.arrow_grammar) — do not hand-edit; run `render_schemas.py canonical-types` after a pin bump. Validating a value directly against this document's URL checks it against the strict canonical_type vocabulary. Type-map regex rules that permit ${name} templates reference the #/$defs/canonical_type_or_template fragment explicitly.

canonical_type.title: canonical_type
canonical_type.description: A canonical type string. Must match one of the Arrow logical type families below — exactly the families the engine executes, per the pinned grammar manifest.

null_type.title: Null
null_type.description: Arrow Null logical type. Column contains only null values. Zero storage.

boolean_type.title: Boolean
boolean_type.description: Arrow Boolean logical type. 1-bit values: true or false.

integer_type.title: Int / UInt
integer_type.description: Arrow signed and unsigned integer logical types at fixed widths of 8, 16, 32, or 64 bits. Width should reflect the source system's declared width — do not upcast `Int32` to `Int64` unless the engine requires it.

floating_type.title: FloatingPoint
floating_type.description: Arrow IEEE-754 floating-point logical types. Float16 is half-precision; Float32 is single-precision; Float64 is double-precision.

binary_type.title: Binary
binary_type.description: Arrow binary logical types. `Binary` is variable-length with 32-bit offsets; `LargeBinary` is variable-length with 64-bit offsets; `FixedSizeBinary(byte_width)` is fixed-length.

string_type.title: Utf8
string_type.description: Arrow UTF-8 string logical types. `Utf8` uses 32-bit offsets (capped ~2 GiB total per array); `LargeUtf8` uses 64-bit offsets.

date_type.title: Date
date_type.description: Arrow Date logical types. `Date32` is days since the Unix epoch; `Date64` is milliseconds since the Unix epoch (must be a multiple of 86_400_000).

time_type.title: Time
time_type.description: Arrow time-of-day logical types. `Time32` supports SECOND and MILLISECOND units; `Time64` supports MICROSECOND and NANOSECOND units.

timestamp_type.title: Timestamp
timestamp_type.description: Arrow Timestamp logical type. Unit is one of SECOND, MILLISECOND, MICROSECOND, NANOSECOND. Timezone is optional; when omitted, the type is zone-naive / local. When present, the timezone is either `null` (explicit zone-naive), an IANA zone (`UTC`, `America/New_York`, `Etc/GMT±N`), or a fixed `±HH:MM` offset. When mapping from a source that declares a zoned type (e.g., Postgres `TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE`), use `UTC` unless there is a source-specific reason otherwise.

duration_type.title: Duration
duration_type.description: Arrow Duration logical type. Elapsed time with unit SECOND, MILLISECOND, MICROSECOND, or NANOSECOND.

decimal_type.title: Decimal
decimal_type.description: Arrow Decimal128 or Decimal256 logical type. `Decimal128(precision, scale)` supports precision 1-38; `Decimal256(precision, scale)` supports precision 1-76. Scale is 0 <= scale <= precision; the scale-vs-precision bound is a cross-parameter rule enforced by the validator API and the contract models, not by these patterns. Use `Decimal128` unless the source precision exceeds 38.

authored_shape_type.title: Authored-shape JSON container
authored_shape_type.description: Bare authored-shape JSON container markers — the vocabulary's ONLY nested-data grammar. `Object` declares a JSON object with a sibling `properties` map describing each child (recursive). `List` declares a JSON array with a sibling `items` field spec describing the element (recursive). `Json` declares an opaque JSON object or array with no inner shape — no `properties` or `items` permitted. Sibling-key enforcement is performed by the validator API and the contract models (`analitiq.contracts`), not by this string vocabulary.

canonical_type_or_template.title: canonical_type_or_template
canonical_type_or_template.description: A canonical type string OR a templated canonical type carrying `${name}` placeholders in parameter positions. Used by type-map regex rules where parameters are substituted from named capture groups (e.g. `Decimal128(${precision}, ${scale})`). Each parameter position accepts either a LITERAL canonical value at its valid range/enum (`38`, `MICROSECOND`, `UTC`) or a `${name}` placeholder; a literal out of range is rejected exactly as the strict vocabulary rejects it, so `Decimal128(999, 0)` and `Time32(NANOSECOND)` do NOT match. A `${name}` placeholder must be a valid identifier (`[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*`), matching the native capture-group naming it resolves from; `${1bad}` / `${ }` do not match. Outside parameter positions, the canonical Arrow base name must appear verbatim — `not an arrow type ${precision}` does not match. Templated branches use `anyOf` (not `oneOf`) because a literal parameterized canonical (e.g. `Timestamp(MICROSECOND, UTC)`) intentionally matches both the strict canonical_type vocabulary AND the templated branch — both readings are correct, and `anyOf` reflects that. This vocabulary mirrors the runtime `_validate_type_map_canonical` shape check plus the placeholder-name rule its sibling validators enforce; a differential parity test pins that alignment.

anyOf.description: Templated Decimal128(precision, scale) — each parameter position accepts its literal grammar (at its valid range/enum, exactly as the strict vocabulary requires) or a `${name}` placeholder.
Templated Decimal256(precision, scale) — each parameter position accepts its literal grammar (at its valid range/enum, exactly as the strict vocabulary requires) or a `${name}` placeholder.
Templated Duration(unit) — each parameter position accepts its literal grammar (at its valid range/enum, exactly as the strict vocabulary requires) or a `${name}` placeholder.
Templated FixedSizeBinary(byte_width) — each parameter position accepts its literal grammar (at its valid range/enum, exactly as the strict vocabulary requires) or a `${name}` placeholder.
Templated Time32(unit) — each parameter position accepts its literal grammar (at its valid range/enum, exactly as the strict vocabulary requires) or a `${name}` placeholder.
Templated Time64(unit) — each parameter position accepts its literal grammar (at its valid range/enum, exactly as the strict vocabulary requires) or a `${name}` placeholder.
Templated Timestamp(unit, tz) — each parameter position accepts its literal grammar (at its valid range/enum, exactly as the strict vocabulary requires) or a `${name}` placeholder.
